engineGuide.h2: Ford Bantam Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford Bantam across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3L Petrol
entries.history: The R2BA Rocam 1.3L powered most UK Bantams with simple, reliable SOHC design for commercial use.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing interference damage - typically 60-80k miles
Rocker cover gasket oil leaks leading to misfires and performance loss
Hydraulic lifter collapse causing tappet noise and reduced compression
entries.cta: Get quotes for R2BA Ford Bantam engine replacement

entries.title: 3L Petrol
entries.history: SFBA denotes post-2006 facelift Rocam 1.3L with revised emissions mapping and improved idle control.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing interference damage - typically 60-80k miles
Water pump failure leading to overheating and cylinder head damage
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting warm-start performance and idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for SFBA Ford Bantam engine replacement

entries.title: 6L Petrol
entries.history: The R4BA Rocam 1.6L delivered higher torque for loaded commercial Bantams, popular with fleet operators.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure from overheating - typically 70-90k miles
Timing belt failure causing valve damage if not replaced on schedule
Crankshaft seal leaks leading to oil loss and clutch contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for R4BA Ford Bantam engine replacement

entries.title: 6L Petrol
entries.history: SFCA is the facelift Rocam 1.6L with updated ECU mapping and catalytic converter compliance for post-2006 UK imports.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure from overheating - typically 70-90k miles
Hydraulic lifter noise from oil starvation or poor maintenance intervals
Exhaust manifold cracking causing MOT emissions failures and performance loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for SFCA Ford Bantam engine replacement

entries.title: 8L Diesel
entries.history: The XLD418 Endura-D 1.8L was Ford's workhorse diesel for early Bantams, durable but limited UK availability after 2006.
commonFailures: Injector pump failure causing hard starting and power loss - typically 80-100k miles
EGR valve clogging leading to rough idle, reduced performance and black smoke
Turbo oil seal leaks causing blue smoke, oil consumption and intercooler fouling
entries.cta: Get quotes for XLD418 Ford Bantam engine replacement

entries.title: 4L Diesel
entries.history: The DV4 DuraTorq 1.4L replaced Endura-D in late Bantams, offering improved emissions and fuel economy for final models.
compatibleVariants: XL 1.4 TDCi, XLT 1.4 TDCi, XLE 1.4 TDCi
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure from oil starvation - typically 90-110k miles
DPF clogging causing reduced power, regeneration issues and warning lights
Injector seal failure leading to fuel dilution, bearing wear and hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV4 Ford Bantam engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Ford Bantam Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Ford Bantam Timing Belt Failure - Why Rocam Petrol Engines Suffer Catastrophic Damage
problems.affectedModels: Ford Bantam XL, XLT, XLE 1.3 (R2BA, SFBA) & 1.6 (R4BA, SFCA) petrol variants (2002-2011)
problems.rootCause: The SOHC Rocam is an interference design; aged rubber belts or seized tensioners snap under sudden load, allowing valves to strike pistons and destroy the valvetrain.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete belt, tensioner, water pump and coolant replacement before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory intervals; essential preventative maintenance if the belt is intact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully rebuilt Ford Bantam Rocam engine with upgraded timing components, bench testing and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Returns the vehicle to like-new reliability; the only viable route once internal collision has occurred.

problems.recommendation: On a Ford Bantam valued at £1,500-£4,000, preventative belt replacement is always more economical than waiting. If your engine has already seized or lost compression from a snapped belt, skip costly repair attempts and opt for a complete Ford Bantam engine replacement to guarantee long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt R2BA Ford Bantam engine prices

problems.h4: Ford Bantam Head Gasket Failure - Why Overheating Warps Cylinder Heads
problems.affectedModels: Ford Bantam 1.3/1.6 Rocam (R2BA, R4BA) & 1.8 Endura-D (XLD418) variants (2002-2009)
problems.rootCause: Stuck thermostats or failing water pumps cause rapid temperature spikes, softening the aluminium head gasket and compromising combustion chamber sealing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, crack testing, precision resurfacing and multi-layer steel gasket installation.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective only if the engine block remains undamaged and cooling system faults are fully rectified.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a tested Ford Bantam engine with new head assembly, upgraded cooling components, bench testing and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates warped metal and hidden coolant passages; recommended when overheating has exceeded safe limits.

problems.recommendation: Head gasket replacement on a Ford Bantam makes sense only if the vehicle was caught cooling quickly and block pressure hasn't warped the casting. If you see milky oil, heavy white exhaust smoke, or repeated overheating, a rebuilt Ford Bantam engine replacement is safer and more cost-effective than repeated gasket attempts.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ford Bantam Rocam engine replacement

problems.group: Ford Bantam Oil Leak Engine Damage
problems.h4: Ford Bantam Oil Leak Engine Damage - Rear Main Seal & Rocker Cover Failure
problems.affectedModels: Ford Bantam 1.3/1.6 Rocam facelift (SFBA, SFCA) variants (2006-2011)
problems.rootCause: Deteriorating rubber/cork seals combined with blocked PCV breather valves increase crankcase pressure, forcing oil past seals and starving the bottom end of lubrication.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rocker cover gasket, rear main seal and oil pan gasket replacement with updated PCV breather installation.
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves external leaks and restores oil pressure; viable if bearing clearances are still within tolerance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt Ford Bantam engine with reinforced seal kits, cleaned oil galleries, bench testing and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary once oil starvation has spun main or big-end bearings; prevents recurring leaks and internal wear.

problems.recommendation: Address Ford Bantam oil leak symptoms immediately with a full seal kit and breather service to protect the crankshaft. If you hear ticking, rattling, or low oil pressure warnings after the leak started, internal scoring is likely underway and a complete Ford Bantam engine replacement is the only reliable fix.
problems.cta: Compare Ford Bantam SFCA engine specialist prices

problems.h4: Ford Bantam Engine Knocking - Why Rocam & DuraTorq Bearings Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Ford Bantam 1.3/1.6 Rocam (R2BA, R4BA) & 1.4 DuraTorq (DV4) variants (2002-2011)
problems.rootCause: Extended oil change intervals or poor-grade lubricants degrade crankshaft bearing coatings, while DV4 diesels additionally suffer from turbo oil feed coking and fuel dilution.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt Ford Bantam engine with upgraded bearing shells, balanced crankshaft, bench testing and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory compression and eliminates bottom-end knock; the only financially sensible solution once bearing failure occurs.

problems.recommendation: Once a Ford Bantam engine knocking symptom is audible, the crankshaft and connecting rods are already compromised. Do not attempt partial bearing replacements; they will fail prematurely under load. Opt for a fully rebuilt unit with hardened bearings to secure long-term commercial use.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV4 Ford Bantam engine prices

problems.h4: Ford Bantam Injector Failure - Why Endura-D & DuraTorq Diesels Lose Compression
problems.affectedModels: Ford Bantam 1.8 Endura-D (XLD418) & 1.4 DuraTorq (DV4) diesel variants (2002-2011)
problems.rootCause: Degraded copper injector seat washers or worn nozzle tips allow raw diesel into the combustion chamber, washing cylinder walls and accelerating piston ring wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, flow testing, nozzle replacement, copper seat renewal and ECU coding.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores atomisation and prevents further fuel wash; viable only if cylinder compression remains above 25 bar.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt Ford Bantam diesel engine with new injector seats, upgraded fuel pump seals, bench testing and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Required when compression loss or heavy blue smoke indicates irreversible ring/cylinder wear from prolonged injector failure.

problems.recommendation: Replace injector seals and recode the ECU promptly to avoid wash-down damage on a Ford Bantam diesel. If your van has lost power, smokes heavily under load, or shows low compression across multiple cylinders, a rebuilt engine is significantly cheaper than machining the block and sourcing new pistons.
problems.cta: Compare Ford Bantam XLD418 diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford Bantam
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you are dealing with a snapped timing belt, blown head gasket, or terminal bottom-end knock, EngineMarket makes it straightforward to compare prices from trusted UK Ford Bantam engine specialists. Every quoted unit arrives fully bench-tested, fitted with upgraded internal components to eliminate the original weakness, and backed by a comprehensive 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide supply and fit delivery, you can bypass main dealer markups and get your commercial van or daily driver back on the road fast.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford Bantam Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Bantam and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ford Bantam Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ford Bantam is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement unit.

items.title: Ford Bantam 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L DuraTorq diesel arrived in late-production Ford Bantams (2009-2011), replacing the older Endura-D unit with improved emissions compliance and fuel economy for final UK-imported examples. This compact four-cylinder turbo diesel delivered adequate torque for commercial use while meeting stricter Euro 4 standards.
commonFailurePoints: injector seal leaks causing fuel dilution
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Bantam 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Bantam 1.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.8L Endura-D diesel powered early Ford Bantam imports (2002-2006), offering robust low-end torque suited to light commercial duties and South African road conditions. Though discontinued after 2006, this simple, mechanically-injected unit remains sought after by UK owners prioritising durability over refinement.
commonFailurePoints: Injector pump wear causing hard starting
EGR valve clogging leading to rough idle
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Bantam 1.8L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Bantam 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L Rocam petrol engine formed the backbone of the Ford Bantam range throughout its UK availability (2002-2011), combining simple SOHC design with low running costs ideal for budget-conscious commercial and private owners. This high-volume unit accounts for approximately 70% of all UK-imported Bantams, making it the most commonly replaced displacement in the network.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing interference damage at 60-80k miles
items.cta: Compare Ford Bantam 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Bantam 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L Rocam petrol option provided increased torque and load-carrying capability for higher-spec Ford Bantam variants (XL, XLT, XLE) across all production years, appealing to owners requiring extra performance for loaded commercial use or towing. While sharing the Rocam architecture with the 1.3L, the larger displacement delivers noticeably stronger mid-range pull without sacrificing the platform's renowned mechanical simplicity. Not sure which engine size your Ford Bantam needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford Bantam specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure from overheating at 70-90k miles

engineYears.h2: Ford Bantam Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Ford Bantam engine changes from 2002-2011, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt engine prices from trusted specialists.

years.description: The original UK-imported Ford Bantam range launched with simple Rocam petrol engines and the rugged Endura-D diesel for commercial users.
keyChanges: First UK availability of South African-built Ford Bantam pickups
Introduction of 1.3L and 1.6L Rocam petrol engines alongside 1.8L Endura-D diesel
Basic, XL, XLT trim levels established for fleet and private buyers
knownFor: Simple SOHC Rocam design enables affordable repairs and wide parts availability
Endura-D diesel prized for low-end torque but limited UK spare parts support
Timing belt critical on all Rocam units - interference design demands strict replacement intervals
High commercial mileage makes head gasket and oil leak failures common by 70k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2005 Ford Bantam replacement engines
years.ticker: Ford Bantam 1.3 Rocam engine replacement • Bantam timing belt failure cost • XL 1.6 rebuilt engine price UK

years.description: The 2006 facelift introduced revised engine calibrations (SFBA, SFCA) and critical wiring/ECU changes affecting compatibility.
keyChanges: Updated Rocam engine management calibrations denoted by SFBA/SFCA codes
Revised wiring harness and sensor layouts affecting ECU compatibility
Introduction of XLE top-spec trim with enhanced equipment levels
Improved emissions compliance for UK MOT requirements
knownFor: SFBA/SFCA engines feature improved idle control but retain Rocam timing belt vulnerability
Wiring harness changes mean pre-2006 ECUs not directly interchangeable without adaptation
XLE trim requires airbag-compatible engine looms and sensor brackets for correct fitment
Hydraulic lifter noise more commonly reported on facelift units due to revised oil gallery design
years.cta: Compare 2006 Ford Bantam facelift engine prices
years.ticker: Ford Bantam SFBA engine compatibility • XLE airbag sensor engine swap • 2006 Bantam wiring harness engine fit

years.description: Mid-cycle production refined the facelift Bantam with stable engine line-up and growing UK replacement demand.
keyChanges: Stable Rocam engine production with consistent SFBA/SFCA specifications
Increased UK importer volume driving higher replacement engine search activity
Minor ancillary updates to power steering pump mounting and AC bracket fitment
knownFor: Proven reliability with routine maintenance but timing belt failures remain the dominant replacement trigger
Rocker cover gasket oil leaks increasingly reported as vehicles exceed 60k miles
Strong aftermarket support for Rocam rebuilds keeps replacement costs affordable for budget-conscious owners
Commercial fleet operators favour XL trim for its no-frills durability and easy parts sourcing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2008 Ford Bantam Rocam engines

years.description: Final production year introduced the compact 1.4L DuraTorq diesel alongside continued Rocam petrol supply for UK imports.
keyChanges: Introduction of DV4 1.4L DuraTorq diesel engine for late-model Bantams
Updated emissions hardware including catalytic converter requirements for post-2009 MOT compliance
Final refinement of Rocam petrol calibrations before end of production
mainEngines: DV4 - 1.4L diesel (XL 1.4 TDCi, XLT 1.4 TDCi, XLE 1.4 TDCi)
knownFor: DV4 diesel offers improved fuel economy but DPF and turbo reliability concerns emerge at higher mileage
Late-model Rocam units benefit from refined manufacturing but retain interference design risks
Catalytic converter fitment critical for MOT compliance on all 2009+ imports
Lowest average mileage examples typically found in this final production year
years.cta: Enquire about 2009 Ford Bantam final-spec engine availability
years.ticker: Ford Bantam DV4 diesel engine replacement • 2009 Bantam catalytic converter engine fit • XLE 1.4 TDCi rebuilt engine price
enquiries: Ford Bantam DV4 diesel engine replacement

years.description: Final UK-imported Ford Bantams feature the most refined Rocam calibrations and limited DV4 diesel availability before production ceased.
keyChanges: Final production year with lowest average mileage examples entering UK replacement market
Continued SFBA/SFCA Rocam supply with DV4 diesel available on limited XLE/XLT trims
No further engineering changes - focus on clearing remaining inventory
mainEngines: DV4 - 1.4L diesel (XLT 1.4 TDCi, XLE 1.4 TDCi - very limited)
knownFor: Lowest mileage replacement candidates but still subject to Rocam timing belt and head gasket vulnerabilities
DV4 diesel units extremely rare in UK - long lead times for verified replacement stock
XLE trim commands premium for rebuilt engines due to airbag and sensor compatibility requirements
Strong enthusiast demand for final-year examples drives competitive pricing on verified low-mileage units
years.cta: Compare final-year Ford Bantam 2010-2011 engine prices

faq.h2: Ford Bantam Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ford Bantam engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Ford Bantam engine prices range from £1,800-£4,200 supply-only, depending on displacement, engine code and trim level. Reconditioned Rocam units offer the best value for budget-conscious owners.
keyPoints: 1.3L Rocam R2BA rebuilt engines typically cost £1,800-£3,200 supply-only
1.6L Rocam R4BA units range £2,000-£3,600 due to higher torque output
Diesel Endura-D/XLD418 replacements cost £2,200-£4,000 reflecting limited UK availability
Supply & fit service adds £400-£800 depending on location and ancillary requirements
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Ford Bantam engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes are compatible with my Ford Bantam?
items.answer: Ford Bantam compatibility depends on exact engine code: R2BA/SFBA for 1.3L petrol, R4BA/SFCA for 1.6L petrol, XLD418 for early 1.8L diesel, DV4 for late 1.4L diesel. Always verify via VIN before ordering.
keyPoints: R2BA and SFBA denote 1.3L Rocam variants with different ECU calibrations
R4BA and SFCA cover 1.6L Rocam engines across pre/post-2006 facelift models
XLD418 Endura-D fits 2002-2006 diesel Bantams; DV4 DuraTorq suits 2009-2011
Wiring harness and sensor layouts differ between pre/post-2006 models
items.cta: Check Ford Bantam engine code compatibility via free VIN lookup

items.question: How long does Ford Bantam engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt Ford Bantam engines typically dispatch within 3-5 working days from UK specialists. Supply & fit bookings require 5-10 days lead time depending on location and fitter availability.
keyPoints: Tested reconditioned Rocam engines usually ready within 3-5 working days
Supply & fit appointments booked 5-10 days ahead based on regional fitter schedules
Emergency replacement for seized engines may qualify for expedited dispatch
Delivery nationwide via secured crate with full transit insurance included
items.cta: Book Ford Bantam engine supply & fit with guaranteed turnaround

items.question: Do replacement Ford Bantam engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt Ford Bantam engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier, so confirm coverage details before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across EngineMarket network
Warranty covers internal components, gaskets, seals and machining defects
Claims processed via UK-based specialist support with no overseas delays
Some suppliers offer extended 24-month options at modest additional cost
items.cta: View Ford Bantam engine warranty options from verified UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a Ford Fiesta Rocam engine in my Bantam?
items.answer: While Ford Fiesta and Bantam share Rocam engine architecture, direct swaps require careful verification of mounting points, wiring harness, ECU compatibility and ancillary brackets. We recommend Bantam-specific engines to avoid installation complications.
keyPoints: Rocam R2BA/R4BA codes appear across Fiesta, Ka and Bantam but with variant-specific calibrations
Mounting points generally align but AC brackets, PS pumps and exhaust manifolds may differ
ECU immobiliser pairing often requires Bantam-specific loom or ECU reprogramming
Using non-Bantam engines risks MOT emissions failure or wiring incompatibility
items.cta: Get quotes for Bantam-specific Rocam engines with guaranteed fitment

items.question: What commonly causes Ford Bantam engine failure?
items.answer: The most frequent Ford Bantam engine failures stem from timing belt snaps (interference design), head gasket breaches from overheating, and oil leaks from rocker cover or crankshaft seals. Early diagnosis prevents catastrophic damage.
keyPoints: Timing belt failure on Rocam engines causes valve-piston collision if not replaced at 60k miles
Overheating from water pump or thermostat failure warps cylinder heads and blows gaskets
Rocker cover gasket leaks allow oil into spark plug wells, causing misfires and coil damage
Hydraulic lifter collapse and crankshaft seal wear accelerate with extended oil change intervals
items.cta: Compare Ford Bantam rebuilt engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: How do I identify which Ford Bantam engine I need?
items.answer: Locate your Ford Bantam engine code on the engine block stamping, V5C registration document, or via free VIN decoder tools. Match the code (R2BA, R4BA, SFBA, SFCA, XLD418, DV4) to ensure correct replacement compatibility.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near timing cover or cylinder head on Rocam units
V5C registration document lists engine capacity and fuel type but not always full code
Free online VIN decoders can cross-reference chassis number to factory engine specification
Trim level (XL, XLT, XLE) affects ancillary compatibility like AC brackets and sensor mounts
items.cta: Use free Ford Bantam engine code checker to verify your exact unit

items.question: Are used Ford Bantam engines a reliable option?
items.answer: Used Ford Bantam engines can be reliable if sourced from verified breakers with compression test reports and documented mileage. However, rebuilt units offer superior longevity and warranty protection for peace of mind.
keyPoints: Request compression test results and service history before purchasing any used Rocam engine
Low-mileage examples from XLE trims often have better maintenance records than fleet XL units
Rebuilt engines include new seals, gaskets and upgraded components addressing known failure points
All EngineMarket suppliers provide minimum 12-month warranty versus limited or no cover on used units
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Ford Bantam engine options with warranty details

items.question: Do you offer nationwide supply and fit for Ford Bantam engines?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists offering nationwide Ford Bantam engine supply & fit services. Mobile fitting available in many regions, with collection/delivery options for remote locations.
keyPoints: Network covers all mainland UK postcodes with accredited Ford Bantam fitting specialists
Mobile fitting vans can attend your home or workplace for convenient installation
Collection service available for vehicles unable to travel to specialist workshops
All fitting quotes include removal of old unit, installation, fluids and basic running-in guidance
items.cta: Find Ford Bantam engine supply & fit specialists in your area

items.question: What is included in a Ford Bantam reconditioned engine?
items.answer: A reconditioned Ford Bantam engine undergoes full strip-down, precision machining, replacement of worn components (pistons, bearings, seals, gaskets), assembly to factory tolerances, and bench testing before dispatch with warranty documentation.
keyPoints: Complete disassembly, cleaning and inspection of all internal components and machined surfaces
Replacement of pistons, rings, bearings, timing components, seals and gaskets as standard
Cylinder head pressure-tested, valves re-seated, camshaft and lifters inspected or renewed
Final bench testing verifies compression, oil pressure and leak-down before crating for dispatch
items.cta: Get detailed specification sheet for Ford Bantam reconditioned engines
